metadata.description: Curated game list for Steam Review Intelligence project. Games selected for analytical diversity across genre, price tier, review sentiment, and lifecycle patterns.

analytical_tags_legend.review_bombed: Game experienced coordinated spike of negative reviews
analytical_tags_legend.full_recovery: Game recovered from negative reception over time
analytical_tags_legend.early_access_success: Early access game that delivered on promises
analytical_tags_legend.early_access_failure: Early access game that failed or collapsed
analytical_tags_legend.live_service: Ongoing service with regular content updates
analytical_tags_legend.annual_release: Part of yearly franchise release cycle
analytical_tags_legend.dlc_heavy: Significant portion of content sold as DLC
analytical_tags_legend.went_f2p: Originally paid, later converted to free-to-play
analytical_tags_legend.late_port: Released significantly later on PC than other platforms
analytical_tags_legend.goty: Game of the Year award winner
analytical_tags_legend.intentionally_bad: Bugs and absurdity are core design features
analytical_tags_legend.visual_novel: Text-heavy narrative game with minimal gameplay
analytical_tags_legend.controversial_dev: Developer involved in controversy unrelated to game quality

games.name: Europa Universalis IV
games.include_reason: DLC-heavy strategy game — tests whether players punish base game for content locked behind paid expansions

games.name: Baldur's Gate 3
games.include_reason: Near-perfect reception on a premium title — positive anchor for price vs review sentiment analysis

games.name: Darkest Dungeon
games.include_reason: Successful early access indie — difficult game with passionate reviews

games.name: Mount & Blade II: Bannerlord
games.include_reason: Long early access period with evolving reviews — good for temporal review pattern analysis

games.name: Satisfactory
games.include_reason: Early access success story with unusually positive reception throughout development

games.name: Euro Truck Simulator 2
games.include_reason: Niche simulation with extremely dedicated audience — tests how passion affects review length and tone

games.name: Cities: Skylines
games.include_reason: City builder beloved by community despite heavy DLC model

games.name: Dark Souls III
games.include_reason: High-difficulty premium game — tests whether low playtime reviews skew negative due to difficulty wall

games.name: Elden Ring
games.include_reason: Same studio as DS3 at higher price — direct comparison for price tier effect on reviews

games.name: Persona 5 Royal
games.include_reason: Long PC port of console classic — tests whether late port players review differently than day-one buyers

games.name: Final Fantasy XIV Online
games.include_reason: Free trial MMO with full recovery from disastrous 1.0 launch — long temporal arc for recovery analysis

games.name: Fallout 4
games.include_reason: Divisive entry in beloved franchise — mixed reviews with strong feelings on both sides

games.name: Wasteland 3
games.include_reason: Niche RPG with solid but not spectacular reception — mid-tier baseline

games.name: Mass Effect Legendary Edition
games.include_reason: Remaster of beloved trilogy — tests how nostalgia affects review sentiment

games.name: Shadow of the Tomb Raider
games.include_reason: Lowest-rated entry in a premium franchise trilogy — price punishment for disappointing sequels

games.name: Democracy 3
games.include_reason: Niche political sim with unusually thoughtful reviews — tests review depth for intellectual games

games.name: Rocket League
games.include_reason: Went F2P mid-lifecycle — reviews split sharply between paid-era and F2P-era players

games.name: Disco Elysium
games.include_reason: Near-perfect game from a studio that imploded — tests whether dev controversy affects review sentiment over time

games.name: BioShock Infinite
games.include_reason: Initially praised then divisively reassessed — tests how review sentiment evolves years after launch

games.name: Tavern Master
games.include_reason: Small indie simulation — baseline for micro-studio reception patterns

games.name: Papers, Please
games.include_reason: Short, acclaimed indie — tests review depth when a game is intentionally brief

games.name: VVVVVV
games.include_reason: Micro-priced indie classic — low price tier anchor for price punishment analysis

games.name: Undertale
games.include_reason: Cult indie phenomenon with overwhelmingly positive reviews — tests whether fan passion inflates review scores

games.name: Cuphead
games.include_reason: Beautiful but brutally difficult indie — tests difficulty wall effect on early-exit reviews

games.name: Left 4 Dead 2
games.include_reason: 15+ year old game with still-active community — long review timeline across lifecycle stages

games.name: Noita
games.include_reason: Deeply complex roguelike — tests whether complex games attract longer, more analytical reviews

games.name: Path of Exile
games.include_reason: F2P ARPG with seasonal content — tests how live service fatigue shows up in reviews over time

games.name: Portal 2
games.include_reason: Near-universally beloved game — high positive anchor for review sentiment baseline

games.name: A Way Out
games.include_reason: Co-op only short game — tests whether short playtime reviews differ for designed-to-be-short experiences

games.name: What Remains of Edith Finch
games.include_reason: 2-hour walking sim with critical acclaim — key test case for low playtime vs positive reviews

games.name: Warsim: The Realm of Aslona
games.include_reason: Solo-dev text-based strategy at micro price — extreme budget tier anchor

games.name: NBA 2K23
games.include_reason: Annual sports release with notorious microtransaction model — tests whether predatory monetization drives review bombing

games.name: Cyberpunk 2077
games.include_reason: Most dramatic launch failure and full recovery on Steam — essential for temporal review bombing analysis

games.name: No Man's Sky
games.include_reason: Most complete review recovery in Steam history — benchmark for recovery arc analysis alongside Cyberpunk

games.name: Hollow Knight
games.include_reason: Near-perfect indie at budget price — tests whether low price reduces critical threshold for players

games.name: Rust
games.include_reason: Notoriously harsh playerbase — tests whether community culture appears in review tone and language

games.name: Phasmophobia
games.include_reason: Solo-dev early access horror success — tests review patterns for unexpectedly successful small games

games.name: The Day Before
games.include_reason: Collapsed 4 days after launch — extreme early_access_failure case. NOTE: removed from Steam store; review API may return limited data.
games.data_warning: Game removed from Steam store Dec 2023. appreviews endpoint may return no data — handle gracefully in fetcher.

games.name: Counter-Strike 2
games.include_reason: Replaced CS:GO — massive playerbase split between satisfied and furious players after forced migration

games.name: Battlefield 2042
games.include_reason: AAA FPS launch failure with sustained negative reception — contrast to Cyberpunk's recovery

games.name: Redfall
games.include_reason: Microsoft AAA failure from acclaimed studio — tests whether studio reputation affects review tone

games.name: The Lord of the Rings: Gollum
games.include_reason: Famously bad licensed AAA game — bad game at mid price, tests price punishment for known failures

games.name: Doki Doki Literature Club!
games.include_reason: Free VN disguised as dating sim — expectation subversion drives unique review patterns

games.name: Goat Simulator
games.include_reason: Intentionally buggy meme game — tests whether humor and self-awareness change how players review bad mechanics

games.name: Surgeon Simulator
games.include_reason: Intentionally bad physics comedy — pair with Goat Simulator for intentionally-bad game review analysis

games.name: Garry's Mod
games.include_reason: 20-year-old sandbox with enormous review volume — long-arc lifecycle analysis and review length patterns

games.name: Helldivers 2
games.include_reason: Cleanest event-driven review bomb in the list: the May 2024 PSN account-linking requirement triggered a sharp, precisely-dated negative spike that reversed within days when Sony backtracked. Distinct from slow launch-failure bombs — a dated event boundary plus recovery, ideal for the review-bombing detection question.

games.name: Overwatch 2
games.include_reason: Launched on Steam Aug 2023 to Overwhelmingly Negative — briefly the worst-rated game on Steam ever, over broken PvE promises and monetization, and did NOT recover (contrast to Helldivers 2). Roughly two-thirds of reviews are non-English (Chinese, post-NetEase), making it a key edge case for language-aware NLP in later phases.
games.data_warning: Review corpus is heavily non-English (majority Simplified Chinese). Any naive English-only sentiment work will mis-handle this game — preserve language field and raw text.

games.name: Starfield
games.include_reason: Big-budget Bethesda RPG that launched Very Positive then slowly decayed to Mixed — a gradual-erosion case, neither bomb nor recovery. Contrast to Cyberpunk/No Man's Sky recoveries and BG3's sustained-positive premium anchor. At 69.99 it also extends the premium price range above the prior 59.99 ceiling for the price-punishment question.

games.name: Stardew Valley
games.include_reason: Solo-developed (ConcernedApe) farming RPG with ~387K reviews at 98% positive — extreme positive anchor at budget price with massive N, and the micro-studio-success counterweight to the AAA failures. Review volume makes it a strong base for review-length and passion analyses.
